2605.07632.title: Post-training makes large language models less human-like
2605.07632.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as surrogates for human participants, but it remains unclear which models best capture human behavior and why. To address this, we introduce Psych-201, a novel dataset that enables us to measure behavioral alignment at scale. We find that post-training -- the stage that turns base models into useful assistants -- consistently reduces alignment with human behavior across model families, sizes, and objectives. Moreover, this misalignment widens in newer model generations even as base models continue to improve. Finally, we find that persona-induction -- a popular technique for eliciting human-like behavior by conditioning models on participant-specific information -- does not improve predictions at the level of individuals. Taken together, our results suggest that the very processes that are currently employed to turn LLMs into useful assistants also make them less accurate models of human behavior.
2605.07632.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作人類參與者的替代品，但尚不清楚哪些模型最能捕捉人類行為及其原因。為了解決這個問題，我們介紹了Psych-201，這是一個新穎的數據集，使我們能夠在大規模上測量行為一致性。我們發現，後訓練階段——將基礎模型轉變為有用助手的階段——在不同模型家族、規模和目標中，始終會降低與人類行為的一致性。此外，這種不一致在更新的模型世代中擴大，即使基礎模型持續改進。最後，我們發現，角色引導——一種通過對模型進行參與者特定信息的條件設定來引發類人行為的流行技術——並未在個體層面改善預測。綜合來看，我們的結果表明，目前用於將LLMs轉變為有用助手的過程，反而使它們成為人類行為的較不準確模型。
